# terrible_netapp_ontap_na_ontap_software_update (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `package_version` (String) Specifies the package version to update ONTAP software to, or to be deleted.

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `download_only` (Boolean) Allows to download image without update.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `force_update` (Boolean) force an update, even if package_version matches what is reported as installed.
- `https` (Boolean) Enable and disable https.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `ignore_validation_warning` (Boolean) Allows the update to continue if warnings are encountered during the validation phase.
- `nodes` (String) List of nodes to be updated, the nodes have to be a part of a HA Pair. Requires ONTAP 9.9 with REST.
- `package_url` (String) Specifies the package URL to download the package. Required when state is present unless the package is already present on disk.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `stabilize_minutes` (Number) Number of minutes that the update should wait after a takeover or giveback is completed. Requires ONTAP 9.8 with REST.
- `state` (String) This module downloads and optionally installs ONTAP software on a cluster. The software package is deleted after a successful installation. If the software package is already present, it is not downloaded and not replaced. When state is absent, the package is deleted from disk.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `validate_after_download` (Boolean) By default validation is not run after download, as it is already done in the update step. For MetroCluster systems, the `download_only` parameter should be run first on one of the sites of the MetroCluster. After the job completes, update the other sites.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `validation_reports` (String) `validation_reports_after_update` as a string, for backward compatibility.
- `validation_reports_after_download` (String) List of validation reports, after downloading the software package. Note that it is different from the validation checks reported after attempting an update.
- `validation_reports_after_updates` (String) List of validation reports, after attemting to update the software package.
